channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Building real-world on-device AI with LiteRT and NPU
item.description: LiteRT is a new framework that enables mobile developers to leverage Neural Processing Units (NPUs) for efficient AI processing, enhancing applications like video and speech recognition. This innovation addresses performance and battery issues associated with traditional processing methods.

item.title: Anthropic admits it dumbed down Claude when trying to make it smarter
item.description: Anthropic acknowledged that recent changes to Claude's system unintentionally degraded its performance, leading to user complaints about lower-quality responses. This highlights the challenges in AI development when balancing improvements with existing capabilities.

item.title: DeleteMe review: A (mostly) hands-off service for cleaning up your digital footprint
item.description: DeleteMe is a service designed to help users remove personal information from the internet, offering a mostly hands-off approach. The review assesses its effectiveness and identifies areas for improvement.

item.title: Dev targeted by sophisticated job scam: 'I let my guard down, and ran the freaking code'
item.description: A developer fell victim to a sophisticated job scam that involved a convincing website and interviews, leading to a security breach. This incident underscores the growing sophistication of online employment scams.

item.title: Solid-state batteries hold more juice, but keep cracking up. Now researchers know why
item.description: Researchers have identified a key reason why solid-state batteries, which promise greater capacity and faster charging, often fail. This discovery could pave the way for improved battery technology in the future.

item.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
item.description: Sierra, an AI customer service startup led by Bret Taylor, has acquired the YC-backed startup Fragment, expanding its capabilities in the AI space. This move signifies ongoing consolidation in the AI industry.

item.title: Claude Opus 4.7 has turned into an overzealous query cop, devs complain
item.description: Developers have expressed frustration with Claude Opus 4.7 due to its increased refusal rate for legitimate queries, as new safeguards intended to prevent misuse are hindering genuine use cases.

item.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
item.description: A ransomware family has been confirmed to be quantum-safe, although its practical benefits remain unclear. This development raises questions about the future of cybersecurity in a quantum computing era.

item.title: This high-tech eye massager makes a great Mother's Day gift - and it's on sale
item.description: The Renpho Eye Massager, a high-tech device designed to relieve headaches and migraines, is currently on sale, making it an attractive gift option. This highlights the intersection of technology and wellness.

item.title: Copilot Chat improvements for pull requests
item.description: GitHub Copilot Chat has introduced enhancements for pull requests, providing richer context and new capabilities for developers working with code diffs. This update aims to improve collaboration and efficiency in coding.

item.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
item.description: Noscroll is an AI bot designed to alleviate doomscrolling by curating content from the internet for users. This innovation aims to help users manage their online consumption more effectively.

item.title: Apple’s Next Chapter, SpaceX and Cursor Strike a Deal, and Palantir’s Controversial Manifesto
item.description: The latest episode of Uncanny Valley discusses Tim Cook's legacy at Apple and the potential implications of his rumored departure for the company's future. This conversation highlights the ongoing evolution of one of the world's leading tech firms.

item.title: Chinese attackers are pwning your infrastructure to use in attacks, 10 countries warn
item.description: A joint advisory from ten countries warns that Chinese cyber attackers are exploiting compromised routers and IoT devices to create proxy networks for further attacks. This highlights the growing threat to global cybersecurity.

item.title: Global pull requests dashboard moves to opt-out public preview
item.description: GitHub's new global pull requests dashboard is transitioning to an opt-out public preview, making it the default experience for all users. This change aims to enhance user interaction with pull requests.

item.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
item.description: OpenAI's latest model, GPT-5.5, powers its Codex application on NVIDIA infrastructure, enhancing developer workflows and pushing the boundaries of knowledge work. This advancement signifies a leap towards innovative AI applications.

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
item.description: OpenAI has released GPT-5.5, which offers enhanced capabilities across various applications, bringing the company closer to developing an AI 'super app'. This model aims to improve user experience and functionality.

item.title: Claude is connecting directly to your personal apps like Spotify, Uber Eats, and TurboTax
item.description: Claude, Anthropic's AI, has expanded its capabilities to connect with personal apps like Spotify and TurboTax, enhancing user experience by integrating various services. This move focuses on personalizing AI interactions.

item.title: At 'AI Coachella,' Stanford Students Line Up to Learn From Silicon Valley Royalty
item.description: Stanford students are flocking to a viral course, CS 153, which features insights from Silicon Valley leaders, reflecting the growing interest in AI and tech education. However, not all students are pleased with the attention.

item.title: US Air Force department names firms to power its bases with mini nukes
item.description: The US Air Force has selected three companies to potentially develop nuclear microreactors at its bases, aiming to enhance energy resilience during grid failures. This initiative represents a significant step in military energy strategy.

item.title: Watch out, Whoop: I'd switch to Google's new Fitbit tracker if these features are true
item.description: Rumors suggest that Google's upcoming Fitbit tracker, possibly called Fitbit Air, may include features that could rival existing products like Whoop. Details remain scarce, but interest is building.

item.title: Samsung Micro RGB vs. QLED vs. OLED: I compared all three and found a surprising winner
item.description: Samsung compared its Micro RGB, QLED, and OLED TVs, revealing distinct advantages and disadvantages for each technology. The findings could influence consumer choices in the TV market.

item.title: Immutable subject claims for GitHub Actions OIDC tokens
item.description: GitHub Actions has introduced immutable identifiers for OIDC tokens, enhancing security for workflows. This update aims to strengthen trust in automated processes.

item.title: Copilot cloud agent fields added to usage metrics
item.description: The Copilot usage metrics API now tracks the use of cloud agents with a new field, improving reporting accuracy for developers. This change reflects GitHub's ongoing enhancements to its tools.

item.title: Dependabot-based dependency graphs for Python
item.description: GitHub's Dependabot now provides more comprehensive dependency graphs for Python projects, improving visibility into transitive dependencies. This feature aids developers in managing project dependencies effectively.

item.title: View and manage agent sessions from issues and projects
item.description: GitHub has introduced a feature allowing users to manage cloud agent sessions directly from issues and projects, enhancing workflow visibility. This integration aims to streamline project management.

item.title: Meta is laying off 10 percent of its staff
item.description: Meta plans to lay off about 10% of its workforce, affecting around 8,000 employees, as part of a restructuring effort. This move follows significant investments and aims to streamline operations.

item.title: Sony WH-1000XM6 vs. Bowers & Wilkins Px8 S2: How I'd justify spending $300 more for headphones
item.description: A comparison between Sony's WH-1000XM6 headphones and Bowers & Wilkins Px8 S2 highlights the value of each based on user needs. The analysis helps consumers decide on premium audio products.

item.title: Built for a hostile internet: Canonical VP of Engineering on Ubuntu 26.04 LTS
item.description: Canonical's VP of Engineering discusses the successful rollout of Rust in Ubuntu 26.04 LTS, emphasizing its resilience against online threats. This reflects Ubuntu's commitment to security and performance.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos breach was humiliating
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Mythos faced a security breach, exposing the model to unauthorized users despite claims of its cybersecurity capabilities. This incident raises concerns about AI security measures.

item.title: I paired headphones to my streaming stick for the first time - and fixed a big TV annoyance
item.description: A guide on connecting Bluetooth headphones to TVs or streaming sticks highlights a solution to common viewing annoyances. This feature enhances the viewing experience for users.

item.title: GPT-5.5 System Card
item.description: The GPT-5.5 System Card outlines the capabilities and features of the latest AI model, highlighting improvements and potential applications. This update is significant as it reflects ongoing advancements in AI technology.

item.title: What is Codex?
item.description: Codex is designed to enhance productivity by automating tasks and integrating tools to produce tangible outputs like documents and dashboards. Its significance lies in its potential to streamline workflows for users.

item.title: Automations
item.description: This article explains how to automate tasks in Codex using schedules and triggers, allowing users to generate reports and summaries without manual intervention. This functionality is crucial for enhancing productivity.

item.title: Plugins and skills
item.description: Learn about utilizing Codex plugins and skills to connect various tools and streamline workflows, improving task automation and results. This is significant for users looking to enhance their productivity.

item.title: Working with Codex
item.description: This article provides guidance on setting up a Codex workspace, managing files, and completing tasks effectively. Its significance lies in helping users maximize the platform's capabilities.

item.title: How to get started with Codex
item.description: A step-by-step guide on getting started with Codex, including project setup and task completion. This is important for new users to quickly familiarize themselves with the platform.

item.title: aletheia-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The aletheia-mcp package has been added to PyPI, enabling researchers to search over 250 million scientific papers and extract full texts. This tool is significant for enhancing literature research efficiency.

item.title: torchlens 1.1.0
item.description: torchlens 1.1.0 is a new package that allows users to extract activations from PyTorch models, which is useful for analyzing model performance. This update is relevant for developers working with AI models.

item.title: useml 0.0.1
item.description: useml 0.0.1 simplifies PyTorch workflows by providing tools for pipeline management, versioning, and vault functionalities. This is significant for developers looking to streamline their workflow processes.

item.title: useml added to PyPI
item.description: The useml package has been added to PyPI, offering features that simplify PyTorch workflows, including pipeline management and versioning. This is important for enhancing user productivity in AI projects.

item.title: hypernix 0.45.3
item.description: HyperNix has released version 0.45.3 of its PyTorch model, allowing users to download and quantize it into various formats including GGUF. This update enhances flexibility for developers working with AI models.

item.title: Bilderberg 2026: The World Elite Gathered in D.C., and Media Refused to Cover It. Here’s What to Know.
item.description: The Bilderberg Group convened in D.C. with influential figures including heads of state and CEOs, yet mainstream media largely ignored the event. This secrecy raises questions about transparency and power dynamics in global governance.

item.title: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Urges Gemini Space Station (NASDAQ:GEMI) Investors with Large Losses to Contact the Firm Regarding Upcoming Lead Plaintiff Deadline
item.description: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. is encouraging Gemini Space Station investors who suffered significant losses to reach out regarding a lead plaintiff deadline for potential litigation. This move indicates ongoing legal scrutiny for the company.

item.title: SpaceX and Cursor have explored a team-up with Mistral to take on AI rivals - Business Insider
item.description: SpaceX and Cursor are considering a partnership with Mistral to enhance their competitive edge in AI. This collaboration could lead to significant advancements in AI technology and market positioning.

item.title: Groundcover adds agentic AI tracing to observability platform, backs Google Vertex AI
item.description: Groundcover has expanded its observability platform to include agentic AI tracing, now compatible with Google Vertex AI. This enhancement allows for better monitoring and management of AI systems.

item.title: rulesync added to PyPI
item.description: rulesync has been added to PyPI, providing a centralized source for syncing AI coding agent rule files. This tool simplifies the management of various AI-related documentation.

item.title: Google launches AI research agents powered by Gemini 3.1 Pro
item.description: Google has launched two new AI research agents, Deep Research and Deep Research Max, powered by Gemini 3.1 Pro, capable of generating detailed research reports. This marks a significant advancement in AI research capabilities.

item.title: This Would be Bitcoin’s Price if Michael Saylor’s Billion Dollar BTC Purchases Did Not Happen – Grok Reveals
item.description: A model estimates Bitcoin's price without Michael Saylor's significant corporate purchases, suggesting a different market landscape. This analysis reflects on the impact of institutional investment on cryptocurrency values.

item.title: hf-inference-acp 0.6.23
item.description: The hf-inference-acp 0.6.23 update introduces an inference agent from Hugging Face with ACP support, enhancing AI capabilities. This update is part of ongoing improvements in AI technology.

item.title: Bring your own Agent to MS Teams
item.description: A new guide shows how to integrate existing AI agents into Microsoft Teams with minimal coding. This simplifies the process for users looking to enhance their Teams experience with AI.
